devanagari: श्रीकृष्णस्य अलौकिकं प्रभावं दृष्ट्वा चकितान् गोपान प्रति नन्दस्य गर्गोक्तिकथनम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । एवंविधानि कर्माणि गोपाः कृष्णस्य वीक्...
english_devnagari: śrīkṛṣṇasya alaukikaṃ prabhāvaṃ dṛṣṭvā cakitān gopāna prati nandasya gargoktikathanam śrīśuka uvāca evaṃvidhāni karmāṇi gopāḥ kṛṣṇasya vīkṣya te atadvīryavidaḥ procuḥ samabhyetya suvismitāḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Witnessing Kṛṣṇa’s miraculous feats of this type (such as lifting up of mount Govardhana) the cowherds, being unaware of his prowess, were extremely astonished and coming to Nanda, they spoke unto him (as follows):

devanagari: बालकस्य यदेतानि कर्माणि अति अद्‍भुतानि वै । कथमर्हत्यसौ जन्म ग्राम्येष्वात्मजुगुप्सितम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: bālakasya yadetāni karmāṇi ati ad‍bhutāni vai kathamarhatyasau janma grāmyeṣvātmajugupsitam

translation: “Verily the exploits of this child are extremely marvellous. How does he deserve to be born among rustic people like us—a birth really unworthy and censurable to him.

devanagari: यः सप्तहायनो बालः करेणैकेन लीलया । कथं बिभ्रद् गिरिवरं पुष्करं गजराडिव ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ saptahāyano bālaḥ kareṇaikena līlayā kathaṃ bibhrad girivaraṃ puṣkaraṃ gajarāḍiva
translation: How is it that a child of seven years as he is, he could hold up with ease (as if in sport) a big mountain on one hand, as an elephant king would do to a lotus (with its trunk)?

devanagari: तोकेनामीलिताक्षेण पूतनाया महौजसः । पीतः स्तनः सह प्राणैः कालेनेव वयस्तनोः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tokenāmīlitākṣeṇa pūtanāyā mahaujasaḥ pītaḥ stanaḥ saha prāṇaiḥ kāleneva vayastanoḥ

translation: Even as the god of death takes life out of the body of a living organism, he, as a babe, with half closed eyes, sucked the breast of the mighty giantess Pūtanā along with her life.

devanagari: हिन्वतोऽधः शयानस्य मास्यस्य चरणावुदक् । अनोऽपतद् विपर्यस्तं रुदतः प्रपदाहतम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: hinvato ano

translation: While a baby of only one month,[1] when he was made to sleep (and was placed) under a cart, he, while crying and kicking up his feet, turned the cart topsy-turvy by the touch of the fore-part of his foot.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Māsyasya—some annotators interpret this as ‘three months old’.

devanagari: एकहायन आसीनो ह्रियमाणो विहायसा । दैत्येन यस्तृणावर्त महन् कण्ठग्रहातुरम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: ekahāyana āsīno hriyamāṇo vihāyasā daityena yastṛṇāvarta mahan kaṇṭhagrahāturam
translation: While he was an infant of one year and was being carried away through the sky by a demon called Tṛṇāvarta, he killed the demon who was strangled to death on account of being caught by the neck by Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: क्वचिद् हैयङ्गवस्तैन्ये मात्रा बद्ध उलूखले । गच्छन् अर्जुनयोर्मध्ये बाहुभ्यां तावपातयत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacid haiyaṅgavastainye mātrā baddha ulūkhale gacchan arjunayormadhye bāhubhyāṃ tāvapātayat
translation: On one occasion, when he was tied to a mortar by his mother for stealing butter, he, while crawling on his hands and feet (along with mortar) between two Arjuna trees, made them fall down uprooted.

devanagari: वने सञ्चारयन् वत्सान् सरामो बालकैर्वृतः । हन्तुकामं बकं दोर्भ्यां मुखतोऽरिमपाटयत् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: vane sañcārayan vatsān sarāmo bālakairvṛtaḥ hantukāmaṃ bakaṃ dorbhyāṃ mukhato

translation: While grazing calves in the forest, he, accompanied by Balarāma and surrounded by cowherd boys, tore open with his hands the bill of the enemy, a demon called Baka (who assumed the form of a heron) with the intention of killing him.

devanagari: वत्सेषु वत्सरूपेण प्रविशन्तं जिघांसया । हत्वा न्यपातयत्तेन कपित्थानि च लीलया ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vatseṣu vatsarūpeṇa praviśantaṃ jighāṃsayā hatvā nyapātayattena kapitthāni ca līlayā
translation: He killed the demon Dhenuka who, assuming the form of a calf, was entering the herd of calves with the desire of killing, and hurling with ease the (demon’s) carcass against a Kapittha tree, caused Kapittha fruits fall down in great number.

devanagari: हत्वा रासभदैतेयं तद्‍बन्धूंश्च बलान्वितः । चक्रे तालवनं क्षेमं परिपक्व फलान्वितम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: hatvā rāsabhadaiteyaṃ tad‍bandhūṃśca balānvitaḥ cakre tālavanaṃ kṣemaṃ paripakva phalānvitam

translation: Accompanied with Balarāma (and possessed of supreme power), he killed the demon in the form of a donkey along with his brethren, he made the forest of palm trees which were laden with abundant ripened fruit, free from fear.

devanagari: प्रलम्बं घातयित्वोग्रं बलेन बलशालिना । अमोचयद् व्रजपशून् गोपांश्चारण्यवह्नितः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: pralambaṃ ghātayitvograṃ balena balaśālinā amocayad vrajapaśūn gopāṃścāraṇyavahnitaḥ
translation: Having made the mighty Balarāma kill the ferocious Pralamba, he rescued the cattle and cowherds in the Vraja from forest conflagration.

devanagari: आशीविषतमाहीन्द्रं दमित्वा विमदं ह्रदात् । प्रसह्योद्वास्य यमुनां चक्रेऽसौ निर्विषोदकाम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: āśīviṣatamāhīndraṃ damitvā vimadaṃ hradāt prasahyodvāsya yamunāṃ cakre

translation: Having subdued the great serpent Kāliya of the most virulent poison, he humiliated his pride and expelled him per force from the deep pool (in the Yamunā) and made the waters of the Yamunā free from poisonous pollution.

devanagari: दुस्त्यजश्चानुरागोऽस्मिन् सर्वेषां नो व्रजौकसाम् । नन्द ते तनयेऽस्मासु तस्याप्यौत्पत्तिकः कथम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: dustyajaścānurāgo nanda te tanaye
translation: Oh Nanda, all of us, the inhabitants of Vraja cherish for this son of yours undissociable affection. And even he bears towards us all a deep love since his birth.

devanagari: क्व सप्तहायनो बालः क्व महाद्रिविधारणम् । ततो नो जायते शङ्का व्रजनाथ तवात्मजे ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: kva saptahāyano bālaḥ kva mahādrividhāraṇam tato no jāyate śaṅkā vrajanātha tavātmaje
translation: A child of seven and the feat of uplifting and holding up a great mountain! How inconceivable it is! Hence we entertain a suspicion (and a fear too) about (the real nature of) your son.”

devanagari: श्रीनन्द उवाच । श्रूयतां मे वचो गोपा व्येतु शङ्का च वोऽर्भके । एनं कुमारमुद्दिश्य गर्गो मे यदुवाच ह ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnanda uvāca śrūyatāṃ me vaco gopā vyetu śaṅkā ca vo enaṃ kumāramuddiśya gargo me yaduvāca ha

translation:
Nanda replied:
“Oh cowherds! Hear from me what the sage Garga predicted to me with reference to this boy. And that will dispel all your doubts or fears.

devanagari: वर्णास्त्रयः किलास्यासन् गृह्णतोऽनुयुगं तनूः । शुक्लो रक्तस्तथा पीत इदानीं कृष्णतां गतः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: varṇāstrayaḥ kilāsyāsan gṛhṇato śuklo raktastathā pīta idānīṃ kṛṣṇatāṃ gataḥ

translation: ‘Assuming different bodies in different yugas, He has manifested himself in bodies of three colours viz. white, red and yellow. Now he has adopted a dark-complexioned body.

devanagari: प्रागयं वसुदेवस्य क्वचित् जातः तवात्मजः । वासुदेव इति श्रीमान् अभिज्ञाः सम्प्रचक्षते ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: prāgayaṃ vasudevasya kvacit jātaḥ tavātmajaḥ vāsudeva iti śrīmān abhijñāḥ sampracakṣate

translation: As this son of yours was at first born of Vasudeva in some place (Mathurā), the knowers will call him distinctly as the son of Vasudeva, the Lord of the goddess Śrī.

devanagari: बहूनि सन्ति नामानि रूपाणि च सुतस्य ते । गुण कर्मानुरूपाणि तान्यहं वेद नो जनाः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: bahūni santi nāmāni rūpāṇi ca sutasya te guṇa karmānurūpāṇi tānyahaṃ veda no janāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Your son has got so many names and forms suitably designating his excellences and deeds that I do not know them all nor do these people at large.

devanagari: एष वः श्रेय आधास्यद् गोपगोकुलनन्दनः । अनेन सर्वदुर्गाणि यूयमञ्जस्तरिष्यथ ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa vaḥ śreya ādhāsyad gopagokulanandanaḥ anena sarvadurgāṇi yūyamañjastariṣyatha

translation: A delight to cowherds and Gokula, he will bring about your weal. And you will easily cross over all difficulties and calamities with his help.

devanagari: पुरानेन व्रजपते साधवो दस्युपीडिताः । अराजके रक्ष्यमाणा जिग्युर्दस्यून्समेधिताः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: purānena vrajapate sādhavo dasyupīḍitāḥ arājake rakṣyamāṇā jigyurdasyūnsamedhitāḥ

translation: Oh Lord of Vraja! Formerly when anarchy prevailed he protected righteous people who were troubled by the miscreant. Through his protection they prospered and subdued the bandits.

devanagari: य एतस्मिन् महाभागाः प्रीतिं कुर्वन्ति मानवाः । नारयोऽभिभवन्त्येतान् विष्णुपक्षानिवासुराः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etasmin mahābhāgāḥ prītiṃ kurvanti mānavāḥ nārayo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

translation: Those extremely fortunate persons who cherish affection to him shall not be defeated by enemies even as the partisans of Viṣṇu at the hands of Asuras.

devanagari: तस्मान् नन्द कुमारोऽयं नारायणसमो गुणैः । श्रिया कीर्त्यानुभावेन तत्कर्मसु न विस्मयः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmān nanda kumāro śriyā kīrtyānubhāvena tatkarmasu na vismayaḥ
translation: Therefore, Oh Nanda, this boy is similar to Lord Nārāyaṇa in excellences, majesty or affluence, glory and prowess. Hence no amazement need be felt at his exploits.’[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Viṣṇu Purāṇa5.13 states that the Gopas directly asked Kṛṣṇa about his feat of lifting up Govardhana, but Kṛṣṇa out of modesty told them that he was one of them and not a superior being.
nāhaṃ devo na gandharvo na yakṣo na ca dānavaḥ /
ahaṃ vo bāndhavo jāto naitaccintyam ito'nyathā //—Viṣṇu Purāṇa 5.13.12

devanagari: इत्यद्धा मां समादिश्य गर्गे च स्वगृहं गते । मन्ये नारायणस्यांशं कृष्णं अक्लिष्टकारिणम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: ityaddhā māṃ samādiśya garge ca svagṛhaṃ gate manye nārāyaṇasyāṃśaṃ kṛṣṇaṃ akliṣṭakāriṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation: Exhorting me thus directly, the sage Garga returned home. Since then I took upon Kṛṣṇa, the remover of all afflictions, as an aṃśa (part manifestation) of Nārāyaṇa.”

devanagari: इति नन्दवचः श्रुत्वा गर्गगीतं व्रजौकसः । दृष्टश्रुतानुभावास्ते कृष्णस्यामिततेजसः । मुदिता नन्दमानर्चुः कृष्णं च गतविस्मयाः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti nandavacaḥ śrutvā gargagītaṃ vrajaukasaḥ dṛṣṭaśrutānubhāvāste kṛṣṇasyāmitatejasaḥ muditā nandamānarcuḥ kṛṣṇaṃ ca gatavismayāḥ
translation: Hearing from Nanda’s speech the report of the account given by Garga about Kṛṣṇa’s glory and having witnessed and heard personally the great prowess of Kṛṣṇa of infinite energy and power, the inhabitants of Vraja became delighted. They respectfully honoured Nanda and ceased to feel any amazement about Kṛṣṇa’s feats.

devanagari: देवे वर्षति यज्ञविप्लवरुषा । वज्रास्मवर्षानिलैः । सीदत्पालपशुस्त्रि आत्मशरणं । दृष्ट्वानुकम्प्युत्स्मयन् । उत्पाट्यैककरेण शैलमबलो । ली...
english_devnagari: deve varṣati yajñaviplavaruṣā vajrāsmavarṣānilaiḥ sīdatpālapaśustri ātmaśaraṇaṃ dṛṣṭvānukampyutsmayan utpāṭyaikakareṇa śailamabalo līlocchilīndhraṃ yathā bibhrad goṣṭhamapānmahendramadabhit prīyānna indro gavām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sad class 1 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third single]
[imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural]

translation:
May that Lord of cows be gracious unto us—the Lord who finding that the Gokula with all the cowherds, cows, and women had resorted to him for shelter as they were afflicted by the driving showers of rain, hail-stones, thunderbolts and tempestuous winds let loose by the deity Indra, wrathful at the destruction of the sacrifice (to be offered to him), took compassion on them, and smilingly uprooted the mountain sportively as one would pluck up a mushroom and holding it up on one hand protected the Gokula and thus crushed pride of Indra.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे षड्विंशोऽध्यायः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe ṣaḍviṃśo
